prose.en: Require the developer of the system, system component, or system service to:
Deliver the system, component, or service with {param:sa-04.05_odp} implemented; and
Use the configurations as the default for any subsequent system, component, or service reinstallation or upgrade.

guidance.en: Examples of security configurations include the U.S. Government Configuration Baseline (USGCB), Security Technical Implementation Guides (STIGs), and any limitations on functions, ports, protocols, and services. Security characteristics can include requiring that default passwords have been changed.

prose.en: the developer of the system, system component, or system service is required to deliver the system, component, or service with {param:sa-04.05_odp} implemented;
the configurations are used as the default for any subsequent system, component, or service reinstallation or upgrade.

objects.en: procedures addressing the integration of security requirements, descriptions, and criteria into the acquisition process
acquisition contracts for the system, system component, or system service
security configurations to be implemented by the developer of the system, system component, or system service
organizational personnel with the responsibility to determine system security requirements
organizational personnel with information security responsibilities
Mechanisms used to verify that the configuration of the system, component, or service is delivered as specified

guidelines.en: security configurations for the system, component, or service are defined;
